metadata.title: SYNGO_BP_750
metadata.comment:
Rationale: NTT4 transports neutral amino acids (submillimolar apparent affinity: proline, leucine and low millimolar apparent affinity: glutamine) in a Na+ dependent, Cl- independent manner.

NTT4/SLC6A17 is a transmembrane protein localized to SVs that is highly homologous to the plasma membrane protein SLC6A15. To study the function of NTT4, a chimeric protein was expressed in HEK293T cells, which had the carboxyl terminus replaced with the corresponding region of SLC6A15 for a greater degree of plasma membrane localization (Fig.2). In this experimental system, the uptake of radiolabeled proline into HEK293T cells could be measured (Fig.3). This transport is dependent of extracellular sodium concentration, pH and membrane potential (Fig.4). The uptake of radiolabeled proline into HEK293T cells is inhibited by neutral amino acids (mainly leucine, methionine, proline, Fig.5).

In PC12 cells, expressed wildtype NTT4 is mainly localized to vesicles and colocalizes with Synaptobrevin-2. Ca2+-dependent exocytosis was induced by ionomycin in the presence of 2.2mM calcium and under this conditions, NTT4-dependent proline transport into PC12 cells was enhanced. These findings indicate that NTT4 is coupled in part to the fusion of vesicles with the plasma membrane. NTT4 may be uniquely relevant during periods of intense neuronal activity.. Experimental description: The coding sequences for the rat isoforms of SLC6A15 (B0AT2/SBAT1) and SLC6A17 (NTT4/XT1) were amplified from rat brain cDNA...
